title: Chapter 10 Quiz: File Handling
description: Test your knowledge of file operations in PHP!

questions.question: Which function reads an entire file into a string?
questions.explanation: file_get_contents() reads the whole file into a string.

questions.question: fgets() reads a file line by line.
questions.explanation: Yes, fgets() reads one line at a time.

questions.question: What does file_put_contents() return on success?
questions.explanation: It returns the number of bytes written, or false on error.

questions.question: Which flag makes file_put_contents() append to the file?
questions.explanation: FILE_APPEND causes the data to be added to the end.

questions.question: fopen() mode 'w' opens a file for reading.
questions.explanation: 'w' is for writing (and truncates).

questions.question: Which function checks if a file exists?
questions.explanation: file_exists() checks existence; is_file() also checks if it's a regular file.

questions.question: How do you get the size of a file in bytes?
questions.explanation: filesize() returns the size in bytes.

questions.question: The function to delete a file is ______.
questions.explanation: unlink() deletes files.

questions.question: rename() can also move a file to a different directory.
questions.explanation: Yes, if the new path is in a different directory, the file is moved.

questions.question: Which function creates a directory?
questions.explanation: mkdir() creates directories.

questions.question: What does scandir() return?
options: A list of files in a directory including . and ..
questions.explanation: scandir() returns an array of all entries including . and ..

questions.question: Which function would you use to find all .txt files in a directory?
questions.explanation: glob() finds pathnames matching a pattern.

questions.question: rmdir() can delete a non-empty directory.
questions.explanation: rmdir() only removes empty directories.

questions.question: What does filemtime() return?
options: The time the file was created
The time the file was last modified as a Unix timestamp
The time the file was last accessed
questions.explanation: filemtime() returns the last modification time as a Unix timestamp.

questions.question: How do you change file permissions to 0644?
questions.explanation: chmod expects an octal number, usually with a leading zero.

questions.question: The permissions 0755 mean the owner can read, write, and execute.
questions.explanation: 7 = read+write+execute, so yes.

questions.question: What will file_put_contents() do if the file already exists?
questions.explanation: By default, it overwrites the existing file unless FILE_APPEND is used.

questions.question: Which mode for fopen() allows appending?
questions.explanation: 'a' opens for writing only, placing the file pointer at the end.

questions.question: fclose() is used to close a file handle opened with fopen().
questions.explanation: Yes, to free resources.

questions.question: What does is_dir() return for a regular file?
questions.explanation: is_dir() returns false for files.
